title: Post-Upload Phase Workflow
summary: The phase after firmware upload completes but before the router has returned to usable operation.
scope: cross-brand concept; model-specific post-upload behavior required before use

phases.title: Wait after upload
phases.purpose: Avoid treating upload completion as immediate recovery completion.
actions: Wait for the profile's documented or observed post-upload duration.
Do not judge failure only from immediate web or ping behavior.

phases.title: Transition back to normal networking
phases.purpose: Handle model-specific requirements such as power cycle or DHCP return.
actions: Switch the client back to DHCP if required.
Use the DHCP gateway as admin URL if required.

readiness_signals.meaning: Firmware transfer completed, but recovery may still be in progress.
readiness_signals.warning: Upload completion does not always mean the router is ready.

readiness_signals.meaning: The client received a gateway IP that may be used for admin UI access.
readiness_signals.warning: Only use this signal when the profile says to return to DHCP.

failure_modes.symptom: Firmware upload completed but the admin UI is not immediately reachable.
likely_causes: Router is still flashing or rebooting internally
Client is still on recovery static IP instead of DHCP
failure_modes.recommended_response: Use model-specific post-upload behavior. Do not tell users to immediately retry or power cycle unless the profile supports it.

panic_entry_queries: firmware upload finished but router not working
router recovery upload complete no web UI

source_refs.notes: ASUS RT-AC86U lab observation motivated explicit post-upload phase modeling.

notes: Draft workflow. App guidance must use model-specific post-upload fields. ASUS RT-AC86U observed post-upload behavior is a supporting example, not universal proof.
